questions.prompt: What is a Glean Assistant Skill?
options: A single saved search query
A reusable package of instructions (and optional templates, examples, and scripts) Glean Assistant can run
A connector to a new data source
A user permission level
questions.explanation: A Skill packages reusable instructions — plus optional templates, examples, and where supported scripts — that Glean Assistant can invoke.

questions.prompt: Who can invoke a Personal Skill?
options: Anyone in the company
Only the creator
Only stewards/admins
The creator's whole team
questions.explanation: A Personal Skill is scoped to its creator; promoting it to a Shared Skill makes it available to a team.

questions.prompt: When Glean auto-selects a Skill, what does it primarily match against?
options: The Skill's step-by-step instructions
The Skill's description / 'when to use' line
The Skill's creation date
The number of times the Skill has run
questions.explanation: Routing matches the request against each Skill's description, so a precise 'when to use' line drives reliable auto-selection — most reliably in Thinking mode.

questions.prompt: Which gives you deterministic control over which Skill runs?
options: Letting Glean auto-route every request
Invoking the Skill explicitly by name or with a /slash command
Rewriting the instructions to be longer
Making the Skill Shared instead of Personal
questions.explanation: Explicit invocation — naming the Skill or using a /slash command — bypasses routing and runs exactly the Skill you named.

questions.prompt: A Skill keeps firing on the wrong requests. What is the most likely fix?
options: Delete and recreate the Skill
Tighten the Skill's description so its 'when to use' boundary doesn't overlap others
Add more steps to the instructions
Switch the Skill from Shared to Personal
questions.explanation: Misroutes are almost always a description problem; sharpen the 'when to use' boundary and retest until the right requests route to the right Skill.
